Taking Payments (Plug & Play)
Things to keep in mind:
You might not have Stripe available in your country, if so there are a few alternatives
mentioned. If you want to use a different payment processor and a different
invoicing system that’s fine just as long as they achieve these things:
1. You can have client’s card on file in order to auto bill them.
2. The client gets sent the invoice straight away.
3. You NEVER use a system where the clients have to pay manually (Except for
the first time)
Step 1: Set Up Stripe
Stripe is by far the best merchant processor in the world. I’ve processed millions
through their system and it’s great.
They charge a 2.9% merchant fee and it takes 2-10 days to arrive in your bank
depending on where you are in the world. TRUST ME though, it’s worth it. The ability
to auto-bill your clients will literally half your stress level as an agency owner.
Once you set up Stripe, it’ll take a few days for you to get accepted. Stripe DOES
allow you to use the platform if you’re under 18 which is amazing. They might ask for
your parents to send in an email confirming they allow you to sign up.
Also - You can sign up to Stripe if you’re just registered as a Sole Trader or a
registered business. To sign up to stripe click HERE.
Alternatives
If Stripe isn’t yet supported in your country, here’s a few alternatives:
1. Square - This merchant is supported in the same countries as Stripe but is a
decent alternative if you don’t want to use Stripe. That said, I still prefer Stripe.
2. GoCardLess - This is a UK only company and once again, might be an option
for some of you outside of Stripe.
3. PayPal - One of the biggest merchants on earth, but one with a lot of
problems usually. PayPal is a great option if you truly can’t use any of the
other merchants but it’s a last resort. They’re known for holding money and
generally having terrible customer service. That said - If you have no other
option then this is still the best option.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Taking Payments (Plug & Play)
Step 2: Sign Up To Simple Invoices
If you want to save money you can skip this step. Stripe allows you to create invoices
and subscriptions directly within the platform, this is free.
I prefer to use Simple Invoices to create the invoices simply because it looks cleaner
and it’s an easier interface to use.
It’s $10 a month and you get a 14-day free trial. Sign up HERE
Step 3: Connect Stripe & Simple Invoices + Add Details
Once you get accepted from Stripe, you’ll need to add to hook up Stripe & Simple
Invoices together.
To do this go:
1. Settings on the left side
2. Payment methods
3. Look for Stripe option, select it and all you have to do to connect the API is
login to Stripe (It’ll open in new tab)
Now you need to add some extra details on the bottom of your invoice as a note.
I shot a quick video to show you how to do that. Click HERE
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Taking Payments From Clients
Step 1: Create new client profile on Simple Invoices.
Step 2: Create new subscription (If it’s recurring) or just new invoice if it’s one time.
Step 3: Make sure you toggle auto-bill & auto-invoice
I made a full video breaking this process down step by step. Click HERE to watch
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
